howItWorks.intro: This push-up test calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Rating based on ACSM (American College of Sports Medicine) norms by age and sex
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How many push-ups should I be able to do?
faq.answer: Varies by age and sex. For a 30-year-old male: 20+ is average, 27+ is good, 36+ is excellent. Standards decrease with age.

faq.question: What is proper push-up form?
faq.answer: Hands slightly wider than shoulders, body in a straight line from head to heels, chest touches the floor, full arm extension at top. No sagging hips or raised butt.

faq.question: How do I increase my push-up count?
faq.answer: Do push-ups 3-4 times per week. Try pyramid sets (1,2,3...10...3,2,1), grease-the-groove (multiple small sets throughout the day), or progressive overload with weighted push-ups.

faq.question: Are push-ups a good fitness test?
faq.answer: Yes — they test upper body strength, core stability, and muscular endurance. Military and fire departments use push-up tests as fitness assessments.
